description_long: This is a large, irregularly shaped stone with a rugged, angular profile and a predominantly grayish-brown coloration. The surface displays a mix of coarse and fine textures, with visible speckling and subtle veining, suggesting a natural sedimentary or metamorphic origin. One side of the stone features a slightly flatter face with lighter patches and embedded mineral fragments, while the opposite side is more jagged and uneven. The edges are rough and unpolished, with some areas appearing chipped or weathered. The stone's mass and size make it suitable as a barrier or obstacle in urban environments such as parks, sidewalks, or landscaped medians. Its overall appearance suggests it has been exposed to the elements, contributing to its natural, weathered look.
description: A large, rugged gray-brown stone with coarse textures, speckling, and visible weathering. Its irregular shape and rough surfaces make it a natural-looking urban barrier.
description_view_0: The broadest face of the stone is visible, showing a flat, speckled surface with subtle veining and lighter mineral inclusions.
description_view_1: A narrow, angular end is shown, highlighting the stone's thickness and a rough, blocky edge with a small metallic marker or embedded object.
description_view_2: The opposite long side is visible, featuring a more jagged, uneven surface with darker coloration and rougher texture.
description_view_3: The other narrow end is shown, displaying a slanted, irregular face with a mix of lighter and darker stone layers.
attribute_location: urban park landscaping, roadside median, or public plaza
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or grouped with other stones on the ground surface
attribute_pattern: Irregular speckling and subtle veining with occasional lighter mineral inclusions; no artificial carvings or regular patterns.
attribute_weathering: Rough and weathered with visible chips and surface erosion, indicating prolonged exposure to outdoor elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends naturally with soil, gravel, or other landscaping stones; may contrast with concrete or metal urban features but integrates visually with naturalistic settings.
